requirement: Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier can create and submit invoices for approved purchase orders.
Invoice can be reviewed and approved by designated approvers.
Approved invoices can be paid by the finance team.
System can generate reports on invoice status, payment history, and other relevant metrics.
System can store and manage invoice attachments, such as receipts and invoices.
Supplier can submit invoices through Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP), SAN/PO Link, AP Mailbox/Invoice Smash (OCR), or Direct Entry.
Supplier can create a PO-backed invoice by flipping a relevant PO into an invoice in CSP or SAN.
Coupa performs auto-matching of PO, GRN, and Invoice for 3-way matching.

Invoice line consists of fields such as Line Number, Line Type, Description, Quantity, UOM, Unit Price, Total Price, PO Line, Contract, Supplier Part Number, Commodity, Period, Billing, Tax, WHT Applicable, and Withholding Taxes.
Invoice fields such as Memo Invoice Field, Car Plate ID, and Inspection Code can be captured.
Invoice can be created via different channels such as Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP), SAN/PO Link, AP Mailbox/Invoice Smash (OCR), or Direct Entry.
AP Team can create a purchase order-backed invoice in Coupa.
Supplier can create a non-PO backed invoice in Coupa and submit it for approval.
AP Team can review and update invoice details in Coupa.
Requester can review and approve invoices in Coupa.
Financial Manager can review and approve invoices in Coupa if required.
AP Team can mark an invoice as paid in Coupa after payment processing.
Coupa receives payment status updates from NetSuite.
Requester can create a non-PO invoice through the invoice form and submit it in draft status.
Accounts Payable can review and update the invoice details, and submit it for approval.
Requester can approve the invoice after reviewing its legitimacy.
Final AP Clerk can approve the invoice before marking it as ready for payment.
NetSuite can process the payment and send the update to Coupa.
Coupa can reflect the final payment status of the invoice.
Supplier can create a credit note from the Purchase Order section in CSP or SAN.
Supplier can fill in the missing details and submit the credit note for review.
AP Team can review the credit note details and reject it if necessary.
AP Team can dispute the credit note and request the supplier to correct it.
AP Team can rectify the credit note in the system and submit it for approval.
AP Team can create a credit note directly in Coupa for PO-backed credit notes.
AP Team can void an approved invoice in Coupa.
AP Team can bulk load invoices in Coupa using a template.
Buyer can create a standard invoice for approved suppliers.
System checks for compliance fields, duplicate invoice numbers, and invoice date before moving an invoice to a draft status.
System generates a Legal PDF and moves an invoice to a processing status during this time.
System holds an invoice if the supplier record is enabled to 'Hold Invoices for AP Review'.
System routes non-PO backed invoices to a named requester user if configured by the supplier.
System holds an invoice if a custom submission blocker approval chain is setup to always evaluate the invoice.
System holds an invoice if the applicable match level is 3-way or 3-way-direct and the invoice is not fully received.
System holds an invoice if the invoice breaches a configured tolerance for its COA or if the supplier record setting 'Always route invoices from this supplier to approval' is enabled.
System holds an invoice if the invoice is from a CSP and not all tax lines are valid or if a submission blocking approval chain is triggered.
System holds an invoice if the billing is invalid or if the assigned requester is not a valid user.
System holds an invoice if the supplier provides a remit-to address that is not mapped to an enterprise remit to address in Coupa.
System moves an invoice to an approved status if all required approvers have approved the invoice.
System allows disputing an invoice in AP Hold, Pending Action, Pending Receipt, On Hold, or Rejected statuses.
System updates the payment status in Coupa after payment is processed by NetSuite.
Invoice can be submitted for approval after passing validation checks.
Invoice can be placed on hold if it requires approval and 'Route to approval without showing Invoice Requires Approval' is unchecked.
Invoice can be booked on hold if the requester enters a new Supplier Remit to Address that is not yet mapped or approved.
Invoice can be disputed by Accounts Payable or Voided by an AP user, and once disputed, it can be Withdrawn or Resolved by the supplier.
Invoice can be voided by an AP user if it is in Disputed status, or by the Admin role if it is in Pending Receipt or Approved status.
Coupa supports matching invoices against purchase orders and receipts to ensure accurate invoicing.
Coupa requires 3-way matching for all suppliers, which involves associating invoices with both a backing purchase order and receipt.
Invoice can have available balances applied as adjustments, which moves it to Approved status.
AP user can select and edit multiple invoices in a sequence without exiting the current screen.
AP user can move to the next invoice to edit after saving or submitting the current invoice.
Coupa runs invoice tolerances to determine whether invoices can be automatically approved or require further review and approval.
Tolerances for Malaysia will be set at 8% SST variance, while other entities will have tolerances set as per specific rules.
An invoice goes on 'Pending Receipt' or 'On hold' status if it does not satisfy matching or invoice tolerance rules.
Coupa provides two types of invoice tolerance settings: Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and Commodity-level tolerances.
Users can set up Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and Commodity-level tolerances in the Coupa system.
Admin can configure submission blockers/warnings for invoices to prevent incorrect submissions.
Users can generate standard reports from Coupa data tables, including filtering, custom views, and scheduled reports.
Power users can generate advanced reports from Coupa tables, including pre-made reports and custom reports.
Users can view dashboards for key performance indicators and real-time statistics.
Coupa compares the manually entered total on the invoice header to the calculated total and displays an error if the two totals are different.
Admin can configure roles and permissions for users, including access to purchase order reports, requisitions, orders, RFQs, suppliers, and items.
Configure invoicing tolerance checks with a '0' value tolerance.
Enable group reporting in USD currency.
Enable central receiving role for exception cases.
